column1: Phelan: Let's see if I can defend my rank better, this time :p
robb.effinger: I just saw your comment in the other game, and I agree..
robb.effinger: I think that you were perhaps being too passive at the start?
Phelan: I think I played some active moves
Phelan: but most were just reactive
C[robb.effinger: With Diplomacy being done, are we down to just one chat?

robb.effinger: (I'm glad that Jamgar said he wanted to concede, since I'm pretty sure that I was going to call that game for France/England no matter what he said.. but I figured I should give him the opportunity to do the right thing and concede on his own)
C[robb.effinger: Hey... are you still running newbie games?
robb.effinger: I may talk a friend into trying out WW
Phelan: I can put the next one into signup soon
Phelan: so it starts soon after the previous one finishes
C[robb.effinger: Thanks Phel. It seems like it may not happen.
C[robb.effinger: Ouch. That move works, doesn't it?
C[Phelan: I think so, that's why I played the G15 hane
C[robb.effinger: I don't don't know the def'n of ""hane"" - piece which wraps around another?
Phelan: diagonal wrapping around another stone
C[robb.effinger: This board always looks so zoomed out after the 9x9
C[robb.effinger: I think this move may be a mistake on my part
Phelan: do you want an undo?
robb.effinger: Eh, no, I'll see how it plays out.. I'm in a bad way this game, gotta take some risks?
C[robb.effinger: Although maybe my way is better than I thought it was... I thought you were going to get the entire bottom left of the board
C[robb.effinger: Oh, that may be a lot worse then I thought :)

C[robb.effinger: Ya, everything is fine... last week was just weird, a combination of busy and sleeping odd hours, and somewhat avoiding online due to WW-drama. And then CastleCon was this weekend, so I was out of town

C[robb.effinger: Hey.. I've been thinking about suggesting a set of meta-rules for WW games, I thought I'd bounce the idea off you first. Something like ""Rated E games"" - so people could fun, for example, a ""Rated E Perfect 11"". The basic premise is that there is low-tolerance for coarse language - no swearing, name calling, etc. First offense gets you a warning, second offence you are replaced, third offense replaced and banned from future rated E games. Thoughts?
C[robb.effinger: Well, I guess we'll see if you're the only one :)
robb.effinger: Good luck in your tourney this weekend!
Phelan: tables really turned on that one.
Phelan: I need to think this one through
C[Phelan: I didn't realize before how weak my groups were.
Phelan: strengthening that connection was much better than saving those stones on the left. :(

robb.effinger: Ya, I don't quite understand how we got into this position either... I seriously considered conceding at a couple of points in this game, such as move 88/J1. And 67/L9 was basically a desperation move.
robb.effinger: So I'm not sure if I'm in a dominating position here? Should we back the game up a couple of turns? I think that your E15 group can live
robb.effinger: How did your tourney go?
C[Phelan: Went well, in terms of event
Phelan: but in terms of games, not that well
Phelan: lost a game I shouldn't have.
Phelan: because I got too cocky.
Phelan: i think your position is much better, yes
C[robb.effinger: Morning, or evening, or something
Phelan: except for the game in front ofme :p
robb.effinger: Ya.. it's 8am here, but I should really get to bed, so... :p
Phelan: you were up till 8 am?
robb.effinger: Sickness seemed to cause random jaw pain one night last week, didn't stay asleep till 10am... still haven't recovered the true meaning of ""day"" and ""night""
C[robb.effinger: So I started my 9x9 game vs Saisays.. I really don't know what to do in that game with a 9 stone deficit
C[Phelan: i had one with adiamant
C[robb.effinger: Opening move: concede felt possibly correct?
Phelan: but I was ready to resign after 3 moves from him

Phelan: you may want to get some sleep :p

robb.effinger: Fair.. I should probably sleep
robb.effinger: I may have found something acceptable
C[robb.effinger: Gah, guess I should have played L12 first
C[robb.effinger: Oh, wait, maybe it doesn't matter
Phelan: thought I had you, for a bit.
robb.effinger: I think that there was a better line for you, if you don't play K11
robb.effinger: I get those 5 stones, but you get the five in the middle, plus the territory?
Phelan: It seemed pretty bad at the time. :p
C[robb.effinger: I don't think I thought that through
robb.effinger: I'm playing while tired again... it was a mistake last time
Phelan: and making time for a timed event
C[Phelan: an event that happens at this time
robb.effinger: Hopefully not one where you need to be alert? :)
Phelan: I'm alert enough for that

Phelan: not sure about the undos yet
Phelan: I'll think about it tomorrow
robb.effinger: Have fun/good luck with your timed event
robb.effinger: So in the new tournament you created, the handicap is set to ""auto""... does that mean we should expect the same 9 stone handicaps that we're currently getting?
robb.effinger: Is there a way to set a better system then that?
Phelan: I don't think there's reduced handicap yet
Phelan: I can possibly remove handi

robb.effinger: It may be more reasonable to set the handicap according to a gentleman's agreement (eg: White will pass for the first X moves, Black will play on specific points? Possibly more trouble than it's worth?)
Phelan: in a tournament, it's probably better to have a system for everyone
C[Phelan: was trying to look for a sequence where you didn't have enough liberties to atari when I played R13 or something like it

robb.effinger: I wondered if that was your plan, and was checking to make sure I did have those liberties...It seems like the cross on the edge is hard to get the advantage on, though.. I should always have at least one liberty from that?
robb.effinger: Good game, thank you as well.
Phelan: well, I could try the ko
Phelan: it gave you an extra liberty
GN[If I haven't moved in a couple days, ping me!]
C[robb.effinger: Thanks. You too. And it's been years since I've played, so any commentary welcome
thd42: Is the 13k more or less accurate as of the last time you played? 🙂

robb.effinger: I think not? I think I was mainly playing on another server (KGS?), and some other folk wanted me to join them over here? I have a recollection of being in single-digit K range, my guess is somewhere between 3-7, but maybe 9?
C[robb.effinger: (But I think the last games I played were on this site - I moved cross-coast and started a new job summer of 2014, which is around when my last games timed out)
C[robb.effinger: (So my reading of this ladder is it's good for me.. am I missing something?)
robb.effinger: Or did you just need to extend it one more time to shore up?

thd42: Suppose this one has been lost for a while

robb.effinger: GGI really couldn't be sure - I felt like I was ahead when I realized that I might be able to kill your group in the top-right, but I also wasn't sure if you were just going to own the entire bottom left.

C[amybot-beginner: I'm a beginner bot, playing 30k-20k games. Suggest a handicap in the game request if it's too hard or too easy. Good luck, have fun!
amybot-beginner: Thank you for the game! If I did something annoying or glitchy - let me know in this chat, in the site chat or by chatting to my administrator. We'll get it sorted.
GN[If I haven't moved in a couple days, ping me!]
C[thd42: Let's try to do better this time 😛
C[thd42: Let's try to do better this time 😛
C[robb.effinger: I think we did fine last time :)

robb.effinger: (Although maybe we could have done a better job discussing the game as it was happening. I was spending a bunch of the end-game wondering ""Am I super-ahead, or am I just 1 mistake away from completely collapsing? Or maybe we're splitting the board and it's really close""
thd42: Did you look at the graph of percentages over time? It thought the game was over when i misread that first cut

C[robb.effinger: Ya, I did.. I don't quite follow the AI's predictions - basically every time someone makes a move the AI thinks they're doing worse than before they made their move? I also haven't played too much, but I played a live game a couple weeks ago where I was massively behind between moves 80 and 271, but then my opponent made one mistake and the game swung 70 points (and 77.4%) in my direction. Hence the ""Am I one mistake away?""
C[robb.effinger: I thought I had a plan for this, and I just can't remember it
robb.effinger: Maybe my last move was bad, and had to handle this. Oh, ya, it was
robb.effinger: I think I was supposed to play N19 last move?
C[robb.effinger: And this seems dangerous, but the alternative seems losing
C[robb.effinger: Hmm. Should I just resign this?
C[thd42: I'm not sure. Could try something drastic first 😛 I do think I'm ahead on points, but The don't ask feel solid to me
thd42: Apologies for being very quiet. I only play on mobile, and there is no notification that you've said anything in the chat unless i scroll down and check
thd42: Happy to chat in more detail over gvc or something at end of game

C[robb.effinger: Ya, no worries, makes sense. It's also useful for me to put in my thoughts as I had them so I could remember them later. Happy to discuss in more detail once the game is done (soon? but maybe this line helps bring me back into things??)
C[robb.effinger: Ya. Oops because the plan was to play S13 first
C[robb.effinger: Okay, I think I am out of things to try :)
C[robb.effinger: Ya, misread the corner @23, since I thought I won the corner after cutting with C3? I don't know why F10 was as terrible as the engine thinks it was?
robb.effinger: Right, and at move 113 I was supposed to play S13, but played those moves out of order. How does that play out if I play it right?
thd42: Want to review over the lunch slot on Friday?
thd42: Hard for me to play out variations on mobile

robb.effinger: Taking suggestions for my opening move... :)

robb.effinger: On the 19x19, against a 1-2 stone handicap, I just kinda play like normal, and assume that my opponent will make a mistake or two to make up the points they started with.. at least, when I have the extra stones, I normally make at least one 10-20 point mistake
robb.effinger: I was certainly willing to concede

robb.effinger: You've got the game, though - you're up at least 20, I think?

C[robb.effinger: And gl, hf to you too, btw

C[rgatti: I'd be curious if you could beat this handicap if I simply randomly placed stones in one of the open spaces next to your previously placed stone (or completely random, if no such open adjacent spaces were available)
rgatti: Even knowing that I was doing that, I think you'd have a difficult time... but maybe a chance
robb.effinger: I feel like my odds certainly go up if that's your algorithm... I can abuse it by placing beside corners and taking the 1/3 that you ""choose"" to place in the corner :)
robb.effinger: And then when I capture your corner stone I have a good shot of capturing the next stone you place as well, since it's either beside 2 of mine or beside 1 and on an edge
robb.effinger: Ya, I think that's doable
robb.effinger: Entirely random may not be, though :p
rgatti: It's just so many extra and well-placed stones in a small space... it doesn't leave you much room to work with
rgatti: I'm not even convinced you can form a living structure... though, Phel conceded before I could confirm that
robb.effinger: I'm not going to lie - if I can, I'm claiming a moral victory
robb.effinger: That is my entire goal this game
robb.effinger: I feel like that was 25 minutes of my life I'm not getting back :p
C[robb.effinger: I, um, think I'm done. Barring objections, it's concession time.

robb.effinger: I felt for a small period in there that maybe this handicap is potentially playable? Maybe if I was a little bit better, I could scheme something out? Or maybe if I approached the early game with more of a plan, rather then ""place stones, hope for mistakes""
robb.effinger: Well, probably more like ""if I was a lot better""
robb.effinger: Also, I'm probably delusional, and I'm just missing the reasons why I can't force things to happen
C[rgatti: If you feel you want to concede, I understand.  Being handicapped almost 5% of the best real estate on the board is far too extreme.
robb.effinger: It's not that I want to, so much as it's that I don't think I have any plays left

rgatti: Thanks.  I enjoyed playing with you, even if it didn't really feel much like an actual Go game.  If you want to play another game (of any size) in the future (with a reasonable handicap), I'd be interested in doing so.
